title: Question 1.1
statement_text: Is it true that for any finite family of graphs $\mathcal{F}$ that contains at least one forest, there exists a constant $k_0 = k_0(\mathcal{F})$ such that $\mathcal{F}$ is $k$-nice for all $k \geq k_0$?
context_text: It is easy to see that if $\mathcal{F}$ contains no forest then it is not $k$-nice for any $k$. The question asks whether a converse holds: every family containing at least one forest is eventually $k$-nice. The paper proves this for all families containing a graph with at most 2 edges and for several two-graph families of 3-edge connected graphs.

title: Conjecture 1.5
statement_text: Any finite family of graphs $\mathcal{F}$ that contains at least one forest is $k$-nice for infinitely many integers $k$.
context_text: This is posed as a weaker version of Question 1.1. Theorem 1.4 shows that $\mathcal{F}_7 = \{K_3, P_4, S_3\}$ is $k$-nice for infinitely many $k$, and Theorem 1.6 gives further support for families containing a star $K_{1,r+1}$.

title: Question 1.8 (Gyárfás)
statement_text: Suppose that $G$ is the union of three triangle factors on the same set of vertices. Is $G$ 5-colorable?
context_text: This question arises in the analysis of the maximum chromatic number of unions of triangle factors, which is the main ingredient in proving item (3) of Theorem 1.3. A positive answer would imply that every 3-regular $n\times n\times n$ 3-partite 3-uniform hypergraph contains a matching of size at least $\lfloor 3n/5 \rfloor$.
paper_contribution: The paper shows that a positive answer follows if either the Molloy–Reed conjecture or the Borodin–Kostochka conjecture is affirmed, and studies the question in the broader context of chromatic numbers of triangle-factor unions (Theorem 1.7).

title: Conjecture 1.10 (Aharoni, Charbit and Howard)
statement_text: In any $d$-regular $n \times n \times n$, 3-partite, 3-uniform hypergraph not containing repeated edges, there exists a matching of size at least $\left\lfloor \dfrac{d-1}{d}\, n \right\rfloor$.
context_text: This is Conjecture 9.3 from Aharoni, Charbit and Howard [1]. The conjecture is motivated by the question of finding large matchings in regular multipartite hypergraphs, and is related via Lemma 1.9 to bounding the chromatic number of unions of triangle factors.
paper_contribution: The paper disproves this conjecture by a large margin, constructing, for arbitrarily large $d$, $d$-regular $n\times n\times n$ 3-partite 3-uniform hypergraphs containing no matching of size larger than $2n/3$.
notes: PDF source — the bound in the statement is rendered as 'd 1/d n' with garbled floor symbols (PDF cid artifacts); reconstructed as $\lfloor (d-1)/d \cdot n \rfloor$ from context and the disproof discussion.
